ministry_department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
short_description: Promotes sustainable agriculture practices through various components including soil health, water conservation, and climate-resilient farming.
detailed_description: NMSA is an umbrella program that seeks to transform Indian agriculture into a climate-resilient production system through suitable adaptation and mitigation measures in the domain of agriculture and allied sectors. The mission has 10 key dimensions including Rainfed Area Development, Soil Health Management, On Farm Water Management, and Climate Change and Sustainable Agriculture. Financial assistance varies by component, ranging from ₹10,000 to ₹50,000 per hectare depending on the intervention.
key_benefits: Financial assistance for soil health improvement (₹10,000-₹15,000/ha)
Support for organic farming and integrated farming systems
other_conditions: Must be a farmer with cultivable land
Land should be suitable for proposed intervention
Must commit to sustainable agriculture practices
Priority given to small and marginal farmers
Assistance varies by component and state implementation

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: 7/12 extract, land records, or lease agreement
For direct benefit transfer of subsidy
For certain components like water conservation structures

application_steps_offline: Contact District Agriculture Office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra
Identify suitable NMSA component based on your land and needs
Obtain application form for specific component
Prepare required documents and project proposal if needed
Submit application to District Agriculture Office
Get technical guidance from agriculture department
Upon approval, implement the intervention with department support
Receive subsidy after verification of work completion

submission_locations.notes: Krishi Vigyan Kendras and Block Agriculture Offices also provide guidance

faq.question: What components are available under NMSA?
faq.answer: Major components include Rainfed Area Development, Soil Health Management, On Farm Water Management, Climate Change adaptation, and Integrated Farming Systems.

faq.question: How much subsidy is provided?
faq.answer: Subsidy varies by component and category of farmer. Small/marginal farmers get higher subsidy (up to 100% in some cases).

faq.question: Can I apply for multiple components?
faq.answer: Yes, farmers can benefit from multiple components if eligible and land is suitable.

common_rejection_reasons: Land not suitable for proposed intervention
tips_for_successful_application: Get soil health card done before applying
Consult with agriculture department for suitable component
Ensure land documents are clear and updated
Follow technical specifications provided by department
Apply early in financial year when budget is available
